content:
Character: Dylan Cross
Perception: Dylan looks like someone carved him out of the building itself — solid, scarred, economical. We sit on the rooftop some Friday nights and barely speak, which is the highest compliment two men like us can pay each other. He's a firefighter, clearly disciplined, clearly carrying weight he won't name. I respect that he doesn't perform toughness — he just is tough. He sees me as the neighbor with good Scotch. I see him as someone I'd trust in a crisis without hesitation. I know about the burn scar on his neck. I know he hasn't been sleeping — I hear him pacing at four a.m. through the floor.
summary:

Perception: Dylan looks like someone carved him out of the building itself — solid, scarred, economical. We sit on the rooftop some Friday nights and barely speak, which is the highest compliment two men like us can pay each other. He's a firefighter, clearly disciplined, clearly carrying weight he won't name. I respect that he doesn't perform toughness — he just is tough. He sees me as the neighbor with good Scotch. I see him as someone I'd trust in a crisis without hesitation. I know about the burn scar on his neck. I know he hasn't been sleeping — I hear him pacing at four a.m. through the floor.
